event: Mesolithic hunter-gatherers became the first humans to settle Ireland after the last Ice Age.
Neolithic farmers introduced agriculture and began building megalithic tombs across the island.
The passage tomb of Newgrange was constructed in the Boyne Valley, predating the pyramids of Giza.
The Bronze Age began in Ireland, marked by sophisticated metalworking and gold ornaments.
Celtic culture and the Iron Age took hold, shaping Ireland's language and society.
Ptolemy of Alexandria produced the earliest known geographic account describing Ireland's peoples and places.
Saint Patrick traditionally began his Christian mission, spreading Christianity throughout Ireland.
Saint Columba founded the monastery of Iona, launching Irish monastic influence across Britain and Europe.
Viking raiders attacked Irish monasteries, beginning centuries of Norse incursions and settlement.
The illuminated Book of Kells was created during the golden age of Irish monastic scholarship.
Vikings established a longphort settlement that grew into the city of Dublin.
High King Brian Boru defeated a Norse-Leinster alliance at the Battle of Clontarf but was killed.
Anglo-Norman knights invaded Ireland, beginning English involvement on the island.
King Henry II of England landed in Ireland and asserted lordship over the country.
The Statutes of Kilkenny sought to prevent the Anglo-Normans from assimilating into Irish culture.
Henry VIII was proclaimed King of Ireland, ending the lordship and advancing the Tudor reconquest.
The Irish defeat at the Battle of Kinsale broke Gaelic resistance to English rule.
The Flight of the Earls saw Gaelic Ulster nobility flee abroad, enabling the Plantation of Ulster.
An Irish Catholic rebellion erupted in Ulster amid grievances over land and religion.
Oliver Cromwell's brutal conquest of Ireland began, including the massacres at Drogheda and Wexford.
William of Orange defeated the Catholic King James II at the Battle of the Boyne.
The Penal Laws were enacted, imposing severe restrictions on Catholics and dissenters.
The United Irishmen led a republican rebellion against British rule that was crushed.
The Act of Union merged Ireland with Great Britain to form the United Kingdom.
Catholic Emancipation, championed by Daniel O'Connell, allowed Catholics to sit in Parliament.
The Great Famine began as potato blight caused mass death and emigration over several years.
The Easter Rising in Dublin proclaimed an Irish Republic and was suppressed by British forces.
The Irish War of Independence began as the First Dail declared independence.
The Anglo-Irish Treaty established the Irish Free State while partitioning Northern Ireland.
The Irish Free State was formally established and a civil war broke out over the treaty terms.
A new constitution renamed the state Ireland (Eire) and asserted full sovereignty.
Ireland declared neutrality during the Second World War, remaining outside the conflict.
Ireland formally became a republic and left the British Commonwealth.
Ireland was admitted to the United Nations as a member state.
The Troubles erupted in Northern Ireland, beginning decades of sectarian violence.
Ireland joined the European Economic Community, the forerunner of the European Union.
Mary Robinson was elected as Ireland's first female president.
A referendum narrowly legalized divorce, reflecting rapid social change.
The Good Friday Agreement brought a peace settlement to Northern Ireland.
Ireland adopted the euro as its currency, joining the eurozone.
The collapse of the Celtic Tiger boom triggered a severe banking and economic crisis.
Ireland accepted an EU-IMF bailout to stabilize its banking sector and public finances.
Ireland became the first country to legalize same-sex marriage by popular referendum.
Voters overturned the constitutional ban on abortion in a landmark referendum.
Ireland preserved the open Irish border under the Northern Ireland Protocol as Brexit took effect.
